notes: ANLI Round 1, few-shot (K=50), from Table H.1 ('Results on All Tasks for All Model Sizes') of the GPT-3 paper itself. In the same table/setting, R2=34.0 and R3=40.2 for the 175B model; R1 is recorded here since the corpus taxonomy has a single 'anli' slug -- matching the exact convention already used for PaLM 540B's anli entry in this corpus (which also picked R1 and noted R2/R3 in the note).

LaMDA 137B, 0-shot, ANLI Round 2 (R2). Same PaLM Table 4 baseline mechanism as the arc-easy entry (footnote 'e' = LaMDA). Verbatim-confirmed via ar5iv render: 'ANLI R2 39.9 e 44.2 ...'. LaMDA also appears as the 0-shot RTE baseline (73.3) in the same table, but RTE has no taxonomy slug so it was omitted. GSM8K-Python pass@1 (7.6%, same paper Table 12) was deliberately NOT mapped to the 'gsm8k' slug -- it's a code-generation variant of GSM8K (write-and-execute Python), a materially different eval protocol from the already-tracked 8-shot CoT gsm8k score, and forcing it into the same slug would conflate variants.
ANLI-R2 natural language inference, zero-shot, reported on the test set. From Table 2 of the primary NVIDIA developer blog (0.366 = 36.6%).
PaLM 540B, 0-shot ANLI-R1 (Round 1), from Table 4. (R2=44.2, R3=45.7 in the same 0-shot setting, not separately recorded here since the taxonomy has a single 'anli' slug.)
